a_image.alt: Outrun style mood board — Sunset Chrome Horizon

sections.a_description: Outrun channels the iconic 1986 arcade racing game aesthetic — an endless sunset highway rendered in hot gradients of orange, coral, magenta, and deep purple. Chrome-finished typography gleams against the dusky horizon while wireframe grids stretch toward a vanishing point, evoking the raw thrill of pixel-perfect speed and palm-tree silhouettes dissolving into a synthwave sky.

This style trades the cool cyan-and-blue neon of generic synthwave for a distinctly warmer palette rooted in the golden hour. Every element radiates sunset heat — from amber-glowing headlines to coral accent buttons — creating an unmistakably retro-futuristic atmosphere that feels like driving into an infinite digital dusk at 200 miles per hour.

Design a web component card inspired by the Outrun aesthetic — the 1986 Sega arcade racing game's visual language of infinite sunset horizons, chrome typography, and vanishing-point wireframe grids. The palette centers on a warm sunset gradient spectrum: deep violet #1a0533 as the base background, transitioning through rich purple #3d1259, hot magenta-pink #e91e8c, blazing coral-orange #ff6a3d, and finishing with bright golden amber #ffb347. Accent with chrome silver #c0c0c0 and white-hot highlights #fff5e6. Typography uses Orbitron (Google Font) for headings — a geometric, squared-off display face that evokes LED dashboards and arcade marquees, capturing the mechanical precision of 80s digital readouts. Set h3 at 28px, font-weight 700, letter-spacing 2px, text-transform uppercase, color #ffb347 golden amber with a text-shadow of 0 0 20px rgba(255,106,61,0.6) for a hot chrome glow. Body text uses Rajdhani (Google Font) — a condensed geometric sans-serif with a technical, futuristic quality that pairs with Orbitron's angularity while remaining readable at smaller sizes. Set paragraphs at 15px, font-weight 500, line-height 1.7, letter-spacing 0px, color #e8c4f0 soft lavender for readability against dark backgrounds. The eyebrow label uses Orbitron at 11px, font-weight 600, letter-spacing 3px, text-transform uppercase, color #ff6a3d blazing coral. Buttons use Rajdhani at 14px, font-weight 700, letter-spacing 1px, text-transform uppercase. Primary button: background linear-gradient(135deg, #ff6a3d, #e91e8c), color #1a0533, border none, border-radius 4px, padding 10px 24px, box-shadow 0 0 15px rgba(233,30,140,0.4). Secondary button: background transparent, color #ffb347, border 1px solid #ffb347, border-radius 4px, padding 10px 24px. Alternative fonts: Audiowide or Exo 2 can substitute Orbitron; Chakra Petch or Play can substitute Rajdhani. The card background should feel like staring into an 80s arcade sunset — use a deep violet base with subtle gradient overlays suggesting the horizon line. All glowing effects should feel warm, not cool — oranges and pinks, never cyan or blue.
